summary: Batch 2 (#3 Tank QR, #7 Audit Trail PDF, #9 Public Stats) end-to-end verified against live preview URL. Backend: 15/15 pytest pass. Frontend: /stats renders 4 KPIs + By Model + By Source + $99/mo coverage block; /tank-qr renders 10 QR cards (Tank 1/12/2/3/4/5/7/8/9/Test 1) with PNG data URLs + visible print button; /compliance CTA button visible with correct href + target=_blank; PDF endpoint returns application/pdf 5256 bytes starting with %PDF-1.3. Regression: /, /free-run, /the-press, /dashboard, /cellar-journal all 200 with no ErrorBoundary trip.
critical_code_review_comments: Stats page uses ownerProcedure for admin.llmStats — works today because of dev-bypass admin injection, but as the agent_to_agent_context_note flags, this must be converted to publicProcedure before real auth lands or /stats will return 401 for unauthenticated visitors.
auditTrailPdf.ts hardcodes the seed-owner-001 lookup (line 43). Acceptable for the bypass world, but leaves a TODO for multi-tenant — should be req.user.id when real auth ships.

TankQr.tsx generates QR PNGs on every items change with an async loop that has no error handling around QRCode.toDataURL — if a tank name produces an encoding error one card failure could halt the whole loop. Wrap individual generation in try/catch.
PDF endpoint has no rate limiting and pulls all entries for the user; with large datasets in production this could be slow / memory heavy. Consider streaming or paginating.
test_credentials: Auth bypassed (seed-owner-001 auto-injected); no credentials needed.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded vintage_log_entries (32 rows, 10 unique tanks).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Live preview: https://ownership-dev.preview.emergentagent.com. Batch 2 regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_batch2_features.py (15 tests). The PDF endpoint is Express (not tRPC) at /api/compliance/audit-trail.pdf?days=N — content-type application/pdf, %PDF magic bytes, ~5KB with current seed data. Stats page is currently behind ownerProcedure but works in dev because the bypass user is role=admin; convert to publicProcedure when real auth lands. Also: console shows scattered 400s on non-tRPC paths (PWA manifest/icons) — not related to Batch 2, ignore.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in this iteration.
